$meta.description: Platform-agnostic design tokens. Single source of truth for web (CSS custom properties) and iOS (Swift constants). Dark-first; a complete, AA-tuned light palette is filled and emitted opt-in (web [data-theme=light], iOS RFColorLight).
$meta.model: Ritmo Studio creator-workstation system. Warm espresso surfaces, copper identity, cyan interaction, plasma peak affect, structured class shape, and beat-derived motion. Builder is calm; Live earns the heat.
$meta.encoding_rule: Color never carries meaning alone. Every meaning-bearing color is paired with a non-color channel (label, number, icon, bar length, position, or weight). Red/green avoidance is a guideline; redundant encoding is the durable rule.
$meta.synthesis: Approved Ritmo Studio design champion. Base: Source B for canonical spacing, schema honesty, responsive behavior, Live isolation, and verification tooling. Grafts: Source A creator voice, campaign scale, share expression, and provenance discipline. Brief-required decisions: capability-honest provider states, explicit 44px selection targets, and authored section labels only when supplied by product data.

color.$comment: Primitive palette. Warm espresso black + copper/orange brand, cyan functional channel, plasma reserved for peak energy only.

copper.$comment: Brand / identity channel. Warm so the intensity ramp grows out of it.

ember.$comment: Hot end of the intensity ramp. Used with redundant label/number/bar.

cyan.$comment: Functional / interactive channel. Controls, links, focus, info. NEVER brand or segment identity. The 700 step is the deep teal used by the light theme so cyan-as-text clears AA on cream.

plasma.$comment: RESERVED peak-energy accent. Allowed only for an All-Out intensity glow, the Live sprint/drop pulse, the Zone 4 kiss in an energy ribbon, and marketing/share artwork derived from actual class peaks. Never a control, link, focus ring, ordinary accent, generic badge, routine planning decoration, or generic brand fill.

amber.$comment: Caution / warning. More yellow + higher chroma than copper. The 600 step is the darker ochre used by the light theme caution graphic (>=3:1 on cream; the paired label text carries AA).

violet.$comment: Recovery/calm accent in the segment track only. Quiet, desaturated.

semantic.$comment: Dark-first semantic roles with complete opt-in light values. Live remains dark in both themes.

live.$comment: Live mode runs darker than bg/base for maximum AAA contrast across a dim studio. Live stays dark even under the light theme (a performance surface for dim rooms), so the light value matches the dark.

primary-hover.$comment: Hover for copper-filled primary actions. Lighter than primary so the fill brightens on hover in both themes.

interactive.$comment: Cyan channel. The ONLY color that signals 'you can act on this'.

peak.$comment: Affect-only. Glow/pulse treatment at maximum energy. Never a fill that carries meaning; the bars/number/label still encode intensity underneath.

state.$comment: Each pairs with a mandatory icon. Color reinforces only. Success is cyan, not green, by design.

caution.$comment: amber-400 (not -500): danger/caution are used as foreground on dark surfaces, where the lighter step has higher contrast. -500 steps are reserved for fills.

intensity.$comment: DB enum is fixed: none/easy/mod/hard/all_out. Display = Zone labels. Warm-to-hot ramp; ALWAYS shown with zone number + bar count + label, never color alone. all_out additionally gets the plasma 'peak.glow' treatment (affect only).

segment.$comment: Current segmentType keys and fallback labels remain schema-honest. A reviewed discipline template or instructor-authored section name may override the visible label; the design system never silently remaps one stored meaning to another. Icon + label lead; tint only reinforces.

move.$comment: Movement vocabulary (the movement-first half of the product). Placed moves (class_track_moves) reference a library move (moves) or a user move (user_moves), or carry a freeform name_override; intensity + anchor_ms position them on a track. Grouped by the REAL moves.template enum (cycle/hiit/sculpt/tread) — not invented. Identity is ICON + LABEL first; moves stay color-neutral (bone) so color never carries move meaning — distinct from cues (copper) and the intensity ramp. Individual move names are DATA in the moves table, not an enum; only the templates are fixed.

ribbon.$comment: Energy-arc ribbon along the timeline — the class's intensity over time as a continuous area graph. Color rides on position+height (height encodes zone), so it stays colorblind-safe. Gradient stops map to the intensity ramp.

family.$comment: Three families, three jobs. Display = brand moments. UI = workhorse prose. Data = the hero numerals (BPM/timecodes) — a characterful mono so the numbers carry the brand.

ios.$comment: Native iOS keeps SF Pro Text for UI while sharing the brand display and data faces.

feature.$comment: Azeret Mono is monospaced so digits are inherently tabular. For a proportional fallback, force tnum.

scale.$comment: px values; iOS maps to points 1:1. Display uses Bricolage Grotesque at normal width with optical sizing; data uses Azeret Mono.

radius.$comment: Larger radius for containers, smaller for controls.

space.$comment: Canonical cross-platform 4px grid. Token keys are grid multiples; web emits --rf-space-{key}, while iOS emits RFSpace.x{point-value}.

surface.$comment: Glass is limited to navigation and transient overlays. Solid stepped surfaces carry dense editing and persistent Live information so contrast and structure remain dependable.

glass.$headerComment: Planning headers are opaque so scrolling content never muddies the layer boundary.
glass.$lightComment: Light-theme glass: warm-white translucent fills, an ink hairline border, and a brighter inner highlight. Emitted under [data-theme=light].

depth.$comment: Resting depth roles. Canvas has no boundary; workbench is sunken with a subtle boundary; contained is raised and bounded only when needed; elevated is transient and may use lifted shadow.

shadow.$comment: Resting surfaces do not cast shadows. card is retained for compatibility but is not applied at rest; lifted is transient. Peak effects remain restricted to real Zone-4 peaks, the Live drop, and marketing/share class-peak artwork.
shadow.$lightComment: Light-theme shadows are softer (lower black alpha on a light ground). peak-glow is theme-independent affect and is not overridden.

gradient.$comment: Brand warmth for marketing/share display text. Copper→ember only; plasma is not a generic brand-gradient stop and remains confined to actual class-peak artwork.

bloom.$comment: Ambient copper warmth behind marketing/share heroes. Plasma is excluded; real peak artwork supplies the rationed plasma moment.
bloom.heat: radial-gradient(60% 55% at 80% 0%, color-mix(in srgb, var(--rf-ember-500) 12%, transparent) 0%, color-mix(in srgb, var(--rf-copper-500) 13%, transparent) 40%, transparent 72%)

tempo.$comment: The signature primitive. Ritmo Studio keeps time. A runtime variable --rf-bpm drives a derived beat duration; pulse treatments sync to it. Used ONLY in Live mode and on the currently-playing/selected track. Never ambient, never in dense planning surfaces. Fully suppressed under reduced-motion.

pulse.$comment: On-beat treatment. Scale + luminance breath, one cycle per beat. Subtle in planning's selected track, pronounced in Live.

tempo.reduced-motion: Pulse is removed entirely; the playing state is shown by a static filled indicator + label.

motion.$comment: Mostly functional, delight in key moments. Respect reduced-motion: durations collapse to 0.01ms, transforms become opacity-only, tempo pulse is suppressed.

signature.track-add: soft slide + snap (snap easing, base duration)
signature.cue-add: brief static highlight on the new marker (fast opacity; no pulse)
signature.the-drop: Live cue advance on All-Out: cross-fade on the beat + plasma glow bloom (onBeat easing). The ONE place real motion budget is spent.

mode.$comment: Contrast and tempo expression diverge by product mode.
